description_long: This object is a preserved crustacean, likely a crayfish or small lobster, displayed on a white rectangular surface. Its exoskeleton is mottled brown and tan, with a segmented body that includes a broad cephalothorax and a tapered abdomen. The crustacean features two prominent claws (chelae) at the front, several pairs of slender walking legs, and visible antennae extending from the head. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front of the cephalothorax. The surface texture appears hard and slightly rough, typical of a dried or preserved exoskeleton. A color calibration cube is placed nearby for scale reference.
description: A preserved brown and tan crustacean with large claws, segmented body, and visible antennae, displayed on a white surface.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the crustacean, with both claws and legs visible, and the color calibration cube to the right.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the symmetrical arrangement of legs and the segmentation of the body, with the cube to the left.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, displaying the other profile of the crustacean and the color cube on the left.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail fan and the alignment of the legs, with the claws less visible and the cube in the background.
